signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh directional call in a defensive macro regime.
ETH is the cleanest long on the sheet because price and open interest are rising together while funding is still flat, giving the best continuation profile without obvious crowding.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable trend continuation, so I would not force a position.
TAO is one of the clearest crowded-upside fades with price up 6.45%, funding at +3.41 bps, and open interest up 24.33%, which makes it vulnerable to a sharp giveback.
FARTCOIN is up 12.18% with positive funding and still-rising open interest, so it remains a strong crowded-upside fade candidate.
NOT is firm but not showing enough structural edge or crowding to justify either chasing it or fading it.
WLD has momentum, but at more than 7% up on the day it already looks too extended to initiate while still lacking a clear rejection.
FET does not have enough liquidity or signal strength yet to deserve a fresh trade despite the mild positive funding.
PUMP is moving, but not in a way that creates a clean continuation or fade entry with conviction.
VVV still leans fade because the move looks more like short-covering extension than durable trend support, but I would keep conviction modest until it prints an actual rejection.
TON is still too strong to short and too extended to chase, so it stays on the watchlist for a future peak rejection.
